metadata.description: Phase 1: UMAP hyperparameter grid search for prefix embeddings. Extending src/hyperparameter_selection.py and building grid search script for the prefix embedding space. Also parallelizing any independent work (projection head, embeddings module prefix support).

decision.description: Use a 2-stage UMAP grid search for prefix embeddings: Stage 1 (coarse) runs 125 configs (5 n_neighbors x 5 min_dist x 5 n_components) with 10 seeds each to narrow to top ~10 configs. Stage 2 (fine) runs top configs with all 30 seeds. Then fine k search on the best config. Uses distance-matrix consensus (not Procrustes) to match the current pipeline methodology.
decision.rationale: Running all 125 configs x 30 seeds = 3750 UMAP fits is expensive. A 2-stage approach (1250 fits coarse + ~300 fine) reduces compute by ~60% while maintaining rigor — the coarse pass identifies the viable region, the fine pass confirms with full seed count. Distance-matrix consensus is used because the handoff doc and codebase confirm it outperforms Procrustes (ARI 0.71 vs 0.56).

context.conversation_snippet: User: proceed with the phase 1 and anything you can feasibly, reliably, and confidently do in parallel

annotation.content: Prefix embeddings with e5-large-v2 produce much higher optimal k than expected. Initial coarse grid search (k range 15-50) showed ALL configs hitting the k=50 ceiling — silhouette still climbing at k=50. Extended coarse k range to [15, 20, ..., 70] and fine k range to [10-80]. First results with expanded range show best_k=55-60 with silhouette >0.96 and ARI >0.90. This is substantially higher than the no-prefix optimal k=28 and even the preliminary prefix estimate of k=37-40 from the handoff doc.

decision.description: Proceed with Phases A-C in parallel: re-extract public probes with prefix embeddings, update full pipeline for final config (nn=36, md=0.01, nc=8, k=35), and update cluster labeling script for k=35. Projection head architecture changed to (1024, 256) per handoff doc recommendation for prefix embeddings.
decision.rationale: Grid search complete with verified results. Final config: nn=36, md=0.01, nc=8, k=35 (min_cluster_size=11). All three phases are independent code updates that can proceed simultaneously, with Phase A (probe extraction) being the only one requiring compute.

context.conversation_snippet: User: yes - proceed with all of this

decision.description: Re-chunk public discourse from raw text_og using 250-word chunks with 25-word overlap, with minimal cleaning (URLs, HTML, whitespace only — NO lemmatization, NO stopword removal). Embed the natural-language chunks with e5-large-v2 + "query: " prefix. This fixes the critical bug where prefix embeddings were generated from full article text (text_phrase_norm) instead of individual chunks, AND the quality issue where over-processed lemmatized text was being fed to a transformer model designed for natural language.
decision.rationale: Two bugs discovered: (1) prefix_comparison.py used text_phrase_norm (full article, 129 unique) instead of chunk-level text (891 unique), causing all chunks from same article to get identical embeddings and artificially inflating all metrics. (2) All existing chunk columns (chunk_text_norm, chunk_text_clean, chunk_text_lexical) are heavily preprocessed (lemmatization turned 'data science' into 'datum science', stopword removal, punctuation stripping created artifacts like 'choirjpg'). Transformer embedding models like e5-large-v2 are trained on natural text and perform best with minimal preprocessing.

context.conversation_snippet: User: i need help figuring out which one of these we should use... this is definitly our chance to make this top tier since we are about to rerun everything once again

annotation.content: CRITICAL BUG: prefix_comparison.py line 206 used text_col_pub = 'text_phrase_norm' which is the FULL ARTICLE TEXT (129 unique values across 891 rows), not the chunk text. This caused all chunks from the same article to receive identical embeddings (cosine similarity = 1.000000). All prefix pipeline results (grid search, k selection, metrics) were invalid — silhouette 0.97 and ARI 0.88 were artifacts of duplicate embeddings. The old no-prefix pipeline was NOT affected (those embeddings were pre-computed correctly from chunk-level text).

context.conversation_snippet: User found identical exemplar texts across different chunks in the same cluster. Investigation confirmed cosine similarity = 1.0 between chunks from same article in prefix embeddings.
